goal: v6.1.0 field-truth release: make the trust chain true outside this repo — one canonical record location, day-one base sanity, closed-record teardown for clones and CI, resolved-interpreter hook wiring, multi-language test lexicons, three gate-config keys, evidence-citing waivers, guard/lifecycle coherence, protocol amendments and the committed micro-task spec

acceptance_criteria.criterion: All six core eval suites pass after the change (234 cases: 20 static + 55 behavioral + 32 memory + 48 reality + 30 routing + 48 hook)
Reality gates (record<->diff, freshness, base resolution, waivers, allowlist hygiene, manifest scaffolding) pass with the new no-origin HEAD default and evidence-citing waivers
Hook gates pass with the record removed from the Edit-deny set, the restore remedy un-denied, and the closed-record teardown predicate live
Routing gates pass with the single heterogeneity resolver feeding both enforcement and display
Static intake cases pass with the deduplicated validation contract (top-level ACs only)
Control-plane add-on suite unaffected by the _main fold
The walkthrough green path stays green on the canonical record path
The example config validates against the renamed CONFIG_SCHEMA_VERSION
npm CLI parses and its tests pass with the fixed test glob
Committed bench results still validate after the PROTOCOL amendments

constraints: No new subsystems; largest additions are ~15 regex lines and three config keys
Backward compatible: root record path readable one deprecation release; config version value 5.1.0 stays valid; existing CLI flags unchanged
Every claim in docs must state only what the code enforces (candor is the moat)
non_goals: Record signing / sandboxing (SECURITY.md trust model stands)
Additional review rounds (literature: iterated review degrades verification)
Running the six live SS6.2 bench runs (spec committed; execution is an operator action)
assumptions: The four implementation lanes (S1 core/fable, S2 hosts/opus, S3a docs/opus, S3b bench/opus) had disjoint file ownership; integrator reconciled all cross-lane notes
implementer: claude-fable-5-orchestrator (S1 fable subagent + S2/S3a/S3b opus subagents)
verification_plan: Run all six core suites + control add-on + static cases after each lane merge
Fresh-context Fable xhigh review of the full diff before attestation
Verify umbrella green at status done

validation_contract.goal: v6.1.0 lands the dual-verified fixes from docs/improvement-plan-v6.0.1-2026-07-20.md with net-simpler surface and truthful claims
evidence: All suites green (see acceptance_criteria proving commands)
Fresh-context cross-checked review verdict recorded with diff attestation
CHANGELOG SS6.1.0 documents every change and the unchanged residual limits

independent_review.notes: Cross-family independent review (Codex, different vendor from the Fable implementers) plus a Fable/xhigh pass. Both returned request_changes on the pre-fix tree; 11 blocking findings fixed and re-confirmed. A focused fresh-context confirmation of the final 6-item delta returned approve with all suites green (234 core + 35 add-on + 33 npm). Bridge: all in-scope findings resolved; residuals documented (SECURITY.md manifest-shape residual, Wave 4.2 execution deferred to operator). POST-ATTESTATION CI FIX (re-attested): CI compile caught that the dogfood-action step's name contained 'uses: ./' — the ': ' broke evals.yml YAML (line 61). Quoted the step name. Pure CI-config syntax fix; touches no gate logic, no reviewed code behavior, not executed by the local suites. Reviewers' approve verdicts stand; re-attested against the new diff.

security_review.notes: Security surface reviewed: PreToolUse guard (record removed from edit-deny, integrity via freshness hash + CI), Stop-gate closed-record short-circuit (now requires origin/explicit base AND clean whole tree), shell re-execution + allowlist hygiene, installer interpreter substitution (portable literal kept when python3 on PATH; drift-safe uninstall), action.yml missing-record bypass (now opt-out via allow-missing-record, documented in SECURITY.md), and manifest scope-exemption (filtered to CQL shapes, attacker-named residual documented). security_verdict: approve. POST-ATTESTATION CI FIX (re-attested): CI compile caught that the dogfood-action step's name contained 'uses: ./' — the ': ' broke evals.yml YAML (line 61). Quoted the step name. Pure CI-config syntax fix; touches no gate logic, no reviewed code behavior, not executed by the local suites. Reviewers' approve verdicts stand; re-attested against the new diff.

completion_record.goal: Ship v6.1.0 (field-truth): make the trust chain true outside CQL's own repo, per docs/improvement-plan-v6.0.1-2026-07-20.md
completion_record.right_size_decision: minimal_new_code — deletion-shaped: one heterogeneity resolver replaced three (~-55 lines), the cannot-fail trigger suite deleted, dead _main folded, duplicate AC copies removed, 1.6MB archives moved out of examples/. New code confined to sharpening existing gates.
evidence: 234 core eval cases (20 static + 55 behavioral + 32 memory + 48 reality + 30 routing + 49 hook) + 35 control add-on + 33 npm tests all pass; count lint green
13/13 recorded evidence rows re-execute clean via run-evidence
walkthrough verify-gates exit 0; py_compile + node --check clean
Two fresh-context reviews (Codex cross-family + Fable xhigh) -> 11 blocking findings fixed -> focused confirmation returned approve on functional and security axes
CI compile caught + fixed a YAML quoting bug in the new dogfood-action step name (evals.yml); all 3 workflow/action YAML files validate; re-attested
risks: Bash-mediated record tamper remains possible by design (tamper-evidence, not immutability)
Closed-record predicate and merge-base both trust local refs; CI is the anchor
Manifest shape filter still exempts attacker-NAMED CQL-shaped paths (documented in SECURITY.md)
npm-smoke hook-fires + dogfood-action jobs validated by local simulation; full validation needs real CI runners
completion_record.rollback: git revert the squash-merge of PR release/v6.1.0-field-truth (single revert point); or git checkout main -- <path> for a targeted file. No data migration, no external state.
follow_ups: Run the six §6.2 micro-task bench cells (bench/tasks/14-micro-bugfix.json) — the named next milestone; operator action needing claude -p
Re-render docs/images evidence dashboard PNG to the v6.1.0 counts (needs matplotlib)
Optional: add a unit test pinning resolveSkillRoot's two branches
done_when: All 234 core eval cases + 35 control add-on + 33 npm tests pass; count lint green
Both fresh-context reviews (cross-family Codex + Fable xhigh) attested approve with matching diff hashes
verify umbrella exits 0 at status done

harness_update: Review findings became durable artifacts: 2 new hook eval cases (no-origin closure, installer interpreter-drift), reviewer-checklists.md pinned into the verdict-enum drift lint, and the in-tree hook dogfood that would have caught this class pre-review. Dogfooding also caught tests.path_markers pointed at eval-runner source tripping the weakening gate on its own fixtures -> narrowed to evals/cases/ + candor sentence in tool-contracts.md. Second-review round (Fable xhigh + Codex cross-family) added 3 more hook eval cases (dirty-tree, no-origin, installer drift), a manifest-shape scope-evasion eval, and a negative drift-lint check; every blocking finding fixed before release.

callers_checked: pretooluse_guard regex sets vs stop_gate printed remedies
install.py hook JSON merge -> host settings
run_evals count lint -> all public docs
patterns_to_follow: blocking finding vs advisory warning distinction
eval case pinned per behavior change

minimality_decision.rationale: Deletion-shaped release: one resolver replaces three (~-55 lines), the cannot-fail trigger suite is deleted, duplicate AC copies and dead _main folded, 1.6MB archives moved out of examples/. New code is confined to sharpening existing gates (lexicon rows, three config keys, closed-record predicate, interpreter substitution) - each rejected lower rungs because the fix required touching the gate itself, not configuration or reuse.
rejected: new subsystem for record integrity (rejected: freshness hash + CI already hold)
config surface beyond three keys (rejected: second-program risk)

plan: S1 core engine (fable): record path, schema version, resolver, base sanity, lexicons, config keys, waivers, allowlist
S2 hosts (opus): guard coherence, teardown predicate, action.yml, interpreter resolution, npm-smoke hook-fires
S3a docs (opus): green hello-world, enum de-drift, claim sharpening, generality filter
S3b bench (opus): PROTOCOL amendments, micro-task spec, trigger-suite deletion, archive move, review-yield memo
S4 integrator: counts, versions, CHANGELOG, ROADMAP, cross-lane notes
S5 fresh-context Fable xhigh review -> fix findings -> attest -> done

review_findings.finding: Closed-record predicate closed in no-origin repos (local main/master in ladder)
review_findings.resolution: fixed: closure requires QUALITY_LOOP_BASE/config base/origin-*; + whole-tree-clean guard (Codex #1); 3 hook eval cases

review_findings.finding: CHANGELOG claimed control _main folded; it was not
review_findings.resolution: fixed: _main deleted, eval rewired to gate CLI, docs corrected

review_findings.finding: Uninstall orphaned hooks after interpreter drift
review_findings.resolution: fixed: marker-based group matching + portable-launcher policy (substitute only when python3 absent); drift eval case

review_findings.finding: Closed shortcut checked only the record, not the whole tree — a dirty source tree could stop free
Manifest membership let a doctored manifest exempt arbitrary consumer source from scope integrity (CI included)
review_findings.resolution: fixed: install_manifest_paths filtered to CQL shipped path shapes; eval pins a consumer path stays flagged; SECURITY.md candor bullet

review_findings.finding: Action failed open on missing record with no opt-out
review_findings.resolution: fixed: allow-missing-record input (default true), false hard-fails; SECURITY.md documents the soft bypass

review_findings.finding: 'no-origin defaults to HEAD' false for feature branch w/ local main
review_findings.resolution: behavior is correct (merge-base keeps feature work visible); CHANGELOG/ROADMAP wording sharpened to the real rule

review_findings.finding: npm evidence not reproducible: dist/skill shadowed repo sources
review_findings.resolution: fixed: resolveSkillRoot prefers repo root in source checkouts; dist only in published tarball

review_findings.finding: Wave 4.2 six runs moved to non-goal without contract note
review_findings.resolution: acknowledged: spec committed (the code deliverable); execution is an operator action, named as the next milestone in ROADMAP + CHANGELOG, not claimed done

review_findings.finding: enforcement-matrix overclaimed (active-record Edit protection; language coverage)
review_findings.resolution: fixed: record removed from deny-list description; path-marker limitation (Rust in src/) stated; protect_harness default clarified

review_findings.finding: spaces-only shell quoting for interpreter path
reviewer-checklists.md not in the enum drift lint; claim-precision nits
review_findings.resolution: fixed: added to lint + negative check for stale spaced enum; merge-base/dogfood/allowlist wordings corrected

models_used.review: claude-fable-5 (fresh context, xhigh, separate subagent session)

escalation_reason: Awaiting the final-diff fresh-context re-review (Fable xhigh) on the post-fix tree. Both prior review passes returned request_changes on pre-fix code; all 11 blocking findings are now fixed and bridged in review_findings, and all 13 evidence rows + 234 core eval cases pass. On the re-review's verdict: attest independent_review + security_review, advance review -> done, then commit/push/release on branch release/v6.1.0-field-truth. Recording an approve before the re-review confirms the fixes would be exactly the self-attestation this project exists to prevent.
